frameworks.name: decoded neural reinforcement
frameworks.one_line: real-time fMRI multivoxel decoding paired with reward to retrain target representations

frameworks.name: implicit/unconscious learning
frameworks.one_line: treatment effect produced without conscious access to the targeted representation

core_question: Can modern neural-decoding methods manipulate unconscious representations to treat fear and related physiological survival processes without engaging conscious awareness?
key_assumptions: unconscious neural representations can be targeted independently of conscious processing
reinforcing decoded neural states changes the underlying representation
fear and other survival circuits share features amenable to the same approach
manipulations: whether the targeted representation is made consciously accessible or not
use of decoded neural-reinforcement vs conventional exposure-based treatment
key_findings: cognitive neuroscience methods including Decoded Neural-Reinforcement can manipulate implicit processes without engaging consciousness
this approach supports double-blind placebo-controlled designs in psychological treatment
the approach can reduce premature patient dropout in fear treatment
interpretations: targeting unconscious representations sidesteps avoidance and dropout that limit exposure-based therapies
the framework may generalize from fear to other physiological survival processes
limitations_noted: generalization beyond fear to other survival processes remains speculative
